title: pachylobus buettneri
intro: Pachylobus buettneri, also known as the Butternut tree, is a species of plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is a tropical tree native to tropical Africa, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves. The bark of the Pachylobus buettneri tree is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat ailments such as malaria and digestive issues.
